trade.summary: Strong trade ties underpinned by cultural and linguistic proximity. Brazil exports agricultural goods, iron ore, and aircraft parts. Spain exports machinery, vehicles, and consumer goods. Spanish companies are major investors in Brazilian telecom and banking.

diplomatic.summary: Brazil and Spain enjoy warm ties rooted in shared Iberian heritage, language proximity, and large diaspora communities in both directions. Spain is an important gateway for Brazil into the EU, and cooperation on cultural and educational matters is extensive.

military.summary: No military alliance. Some defense cooperation through NATO-Brazil contacts and occasional exercises. Spain has sold patrol vessels to Brazil.

financial.summary: Spain is one of the largest foreign investors in Brazil. Santander, Telefónica (Vivo), and Iberdrola have major operations in Brazil. Spanish FDI spans banking, telecom, energy, and infrastructure.

energy.summary: Spain imports some Brazilian ethanol. Iberdrola's Brazilian subsidiary (Neoenergia) is a major electricity operator. Cooperation on renewable energy growing.

scientific.summary: Active scientific and educational exchanges. Large Brazilian student community in Spain. Joint research programs in marine science, social sciences, and humanities.
